Title: Leave blank to Auto-calculate - Blank Transaction Number - Blank Estimate Number - Blank Invoice Number
Description:
The customer contacted us because their estimate numbers were not populating even though the screen says that if we leave the RefNumber field empty the number should auto calculate. 

Unfortunately this is not the case because the transaction could:

1.) Be wait for sync approval (and method numbering is turned off)
2.) Custom Numbering Might be on in QuickBooks
3.) The Transaction might be in conflict
Solution: Unfortunately, at this time, there is no easy way to customize a warning to the user explaining why their transaction is not having a number assigned.

Title: Email Transactions - Option to Edit Emails before sending
Description:
Many customers have requested we have the ability to edit the template that will be sent off to the customer from a transaction screen. This will allow them to quickly modify the transaction and personalize it to each customer prior to sending it out. It should also allow them to edit the to/ cc/ bcc fields per transactions. 

Please quote how long it would take to implement:

Hello, recently we got the following feedback from account, cmisales:
'This particular issue has been raised by me since before I signed up for Method. The absolute poor integration of email services within the product. There was a ray of hope/progress on Sept 27 when the email service for invoices allowed cc and bcc fields to be accessed.

My need is very simple.
When I want to email a quote, or invoice, or packing slip from a button on one of these screens, I want to transfer control to the method email app that has

the send-to field populated from the invoice contact,

the subject line populated with some standard text along with the quote/invoice/packing slip number. ie....“CMI  xxxxxx”

and the pdf of the quote/invoice/packing slip attached.
With the email app now in control I can choose to change/add send-to addresses, bcc,cc, subject, body, and attachments.

Why isn’t this a standard feature?'

Basically, they want to be able to edit what is being sent for a transaction, before it is sent. 

this is actually a pretty common request, i've always pointed them to PS. 
Whats requested commonly actually is to change the email address the email is going to

so i proposed this feature: 

When the 'Are you sure?' pop up comes up before you send an email from a transaction, it would be cool if there was a button there to click if you'd like to make a quick edit before sending.
A button that will direct you to the send email screen, where the email that was abut to be sent is autoloaded into the fields. 

From there, you can change the fields and send the email from this screen with the changes
Thanks!!!
Solution:
Zach has graciously created a catalog entry for this request.

This allows you to select contacts to email from transactions
Edit the contacts and add new contacts.
Allows you to edit the body of the email.
Allows you to also attach documents and bcc or cc anyone you need.

Please note, that the billable time to implement this is estimate at 5 hours since there are multiple considerations and tests.

Title: Customer Job App - Sub Customer App - Contacts App Improvement
Description:
Customer is looking for an app that organizes their sub-customers or customer jobs all in one central location. 

QuickBooks, indents the jobs and subs of a customers; however, Method just serves this up as one big list.
Making it difficult to find or manage jobs.

Keywords:
Subcustomers - Job of Customer - Sub Customer - View
Solution: This app is for users who want to separate their Companies from their Jobs. The Companies List screen displays a list of only parent Customer records. There is also a View Company screen included which has a list of Contacts and a list of Jobs for the active Company (Customer) record. Each of these grids has a row click into the Contacts app.

Title: Show Customer Balance Update in Realtime - Customer / Invoice Balance Not Updating after Payment
Description: My client's balance on the invoice screen is showing 0.00 (I was the one who took his payment so I know he's all set) but his balance under the Customer Details is showing that he still owes the invoice amount. He will also see this when he logs into his portal which is incorrect. Can you please advise?
Solution:
The customer balance requires an active QuickBooks Connection to be updated at this time:

1.) The customer should check that QuickBooks is connected
2.) The customer should check if the Customer, Invoice, and Payment are not in conflict
3.) Lastly, if everything is working the Customer Balance should update in about 15 seconds.

We certainly can and should improve this experience; however, customization won't be a fool proof solution.

Title: Estimate Reminder Improvement - Automated Reminders - Followup Activities
Description:
Customer would like to automate an activity reminder for open or pending estimates based on 

1.) Expiration Date 
2.) Age of Estimate

Ideally, based on a few conditions, they would like:
1.) An Activity to be assigned to the rep  for a phone call FU
2.) An Automated email reminder for the customer

Keyworkds:
Estimate Reminders
Estimate Automation
Estimate email
Estimate Automate
Solution:
Absolutely, customizable.

1.) We would first need to establish the rules for the automation, do we want this to occur every 'x' days or do we want it to occur once, it passes a threshold. Maybe both?

2.) From there creating the email and activity should be straight forward.

An example where this customization exists today is in Alocet where we have an automated email reminder 3, 7, and 14 days after a proposal is sent. On the 21st day, we send a notification to the Rep on file to let them know that the proposal has expired and followup with personal phone call / email.

2.) The customer can upload the attachment to the Estimate, and email the estimate & attachment together. We could also automate uploading / attaching this document to every estimate.

Title: Mass Delete / Batch Delete / Mass Update / Batch Update
Description:
The customer would like a batch delete button for activities; however, this customization is common for a number of functions. 

We have a helpcentre article on the process; how long should we expect this functionality to take?

Support for updating records in bulk.

Keywords:
Bulk Updating
Solution:
Mass Deletes / Batch Deletes
- Are generally not recommended. 
- For activities we can just Batch Update Cancellation. 
- However, in some circumstances we understand this is necessary. 


Mass Updates / Batch Updates
- They generally take the same amount of time.
- Generally the process only gets complicated when we have to implement business rules.
- For example, only update customers that are active with an open estimate.
- Only update activities in the future not the past.

Title: Item Pictures - Add Picture to Line Item on Transactions
Description: We have received requests to add a picture per (specified) items so the visual is available when the SalesRep selects the item on Invoice/Estimates.
Solution:
Note: The number of items wont add to the time, because we can set it up so they can add the images themselves

2 Workflows:

1. Add a text field
2. Track the URL where the image has been uploaded (custom website etc.)
3. Use HTML to render the image on screen / in a report

----

1. Add picture Field to the item table to allow for image uploads on items
2. Expose picture field on Inventory, Non-Inventory, Service item screens
3. Add a button on the editable grid to show message - Show message retrieves image and displays it when button is clicked

Title: Add Credit Card Processing Fee to Invoices.
Description:
Currently, the ability to add a convenience fee for paying by credit card is not available in QuickBooks Online. As a workaround, you would need to create a service item for the credit card convenience fee and manually add it as a line item on your customer's invoice to indicate the fee.

Can this be done automatically if a customer choose CC payment on Method Portal?

If this is not possible then can we add something like how we have in our Donor Page App?
Solution:
Yes this can be done in the Customer Portal for when a Customer is paying off an Invoice. 

First we would need a table to hold all the possible Credit Cards that they accept. This table should hold fields like Name, Percentage Amount, Is Active. I would recommend being able to work with this table in either the Invoice Preferences or Payment Preferences.

Next we would need to have this table appear on Guest - View Invoice. We would show a dropdown where they can select which Credit Card they are going to use. On Apply, this will update the Invoice to show a new Invoice Line for the Credit Card charge. The Percentage Amount will work with the Invoice's Subtotal to come up with it's Rate. We would need to add screen logic to Show/Hide these payment options based off if a Credit Card charge has already been applied or if the Balance Remaining = 0.

If you want to apply this to do a Payment for your entire balance or for a bunch of selected Invoices off the Guest - Transaction List screen, this will balloon the time estimate.

Title: Packing Slips - The ability to Create a Packing Slip for a Transaction
Description:
The customer is just looking to convert an existing Estimate, Invoice, Sales Order or Work Order into an itemized list without any pricing.

This document would be used to attach with shipping to ensure the list of items is included.


Keywords:
Delivery Notes
Shipping Information
Delivery Info
Solution:
The solution here is quite simple, we would:

1.) Create a new button called Print Packing Slip
2.) Create a preference to keep track of the Packing Slip Template
3.) Create a new PDF template based on the transaction and remove all $ amounts from the pdf.

Title: Simple Price Rules / Price Levels (Method Only)
Description:
Custom Method Only Price Rules

1.) Fixed Percentage (% discount on everything)
2.) Quanity Based Discount (based on volume get 'x' price)
3.) Customer Specific Pricing (based on customer & item combination)

*** Note the customer will input pricing and price levels based on their need.
Solution:
Price Rules are complicated

This estimate assumes:
1.) They are not using Price Levels in QBDT 
2.) They are not using Price Rules in QBO (equivalent to Price Levels in QBDT) - We don't sync with these. 

===================

Price Rules can be very basic (Option 1)
1.) Create a custom table where the rules can be managed
2.) Create a screen where the rules can be edited
3.) Setup the format for the rules -- Fixed Percentage (5%, 10% or 20%)
4.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created.
5.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event. 

===================

Price Rules based on Quantity (Option 2)
1.) Create a sub-table to the item table 
2.) Setup the different levels and pricing for the item (0-5, 6-10, 10-20, 20+) 
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the quatity and apply the correct sales price
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

===================

Price Rules based on Item & Customer (Option 3)
1.) Create a sub-table to the customer table with a link to the item table 
2.) Setup the different items  and pricing for the item for the customer
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the custom table see if the selected customer has specialized pricing use that sales price. Else, we'd default to the default item sales price. 
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

Title: Show Customer Balance Update in Realtime - Customer / Invoice Balance Not Updating after Payment
Description: My client's balance on the invoice screen is showing 0.00 (I was the one who took his payment so I know he's all set) but his balance under the Customer Details is showing that he still owes the invoice amount. He will also see this when he logs into his portal which is incorrect. Can you please advise?
Solution:
The customer balance requires an active QuickBooks Connection to be updated at this time:

1.) The customer should check that QuickBooks is connected
2.) The customer should check if the Customer, Invoice, and Payment are not in conflict
3.) Lastly, if everything is working the Customer Balance should update in about 15 seconds.

We certainly can and should improve this experience; however, customization won't be a fool proof solution.

Title: Estimate Reminder Improvement - Automated Reminders - Followup Activities
Description:
Customer would like to automate an activity reminder for open or pending estimates based on 

1.) Expiration Date 
2.) Age of Estimate

Ideally, based on a few conditions, they would like:
1.) An Activity to be assigned to the rep  for a phone call FU
2.) An Automated email reminder for the customer

Keyworkds:
Estimate Reminders
Estimate Automation
Estimate email
Estimate Automate
Solution:
Absolutely, customizable.

1.) We would first need to establish the rules for the automation, do we want this to occur every 'x' days or do we want it to occur once, it passes a threshold. Maybe both?

2.) From there creating the email and activity should be straight forward.

An example where this customization exists today is in Alocet where we have an automated email reminder 3, 7, and 14 days after a proposal is sent. On the 21st day, we send a notification to the Rep on file to let them know that the proposal has expired and followup with personal phone call / email.

Title: Simple Price Rules / Price Levels (Method Only)
Description:
Custom Method Only Price Rules

1.) Fixed Percentage (% discount on everything)
2.) Quanity Based Discount (based on volume get 'x' price)
3.) Customer Specific Pricing (based on customer & item combination)

*** Note the customer will input pricing and price levels based on their need.
Solution:
Price Rules are complicated

This estimate assumes:
1.) They are not using Price Levels in QBDT 
2.) They are not using Price Rules in QBO (equivalent to Price Levels in QBDT) - We don't sync with these. 

===================

Price Rules can be very basic (Option 1)
1.) Create a custom table where the rules can be managed
2.) Create a screen where the rules can be edited
3.) Setup the format for the rules -- Fixed Percentage (5%, 10% or 20%)
4.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created.
5.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event. 

===================

Price Rules based on Quantity (Option 2)
1.) Create a sub-table to the item table 
2.) Setup the different levels and pricing for the item (0-5, 6-10, 10-20, 20+) 
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the quatity and apply the correct sales price
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

===================

Price Rules based on Item & Customer (Option 3)
1.) Create a sub-table to the customer table with a link to the item table 
2.) Setup the different items  and pricing for the item for the customer
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the custom table see if the selected customer has specialized pricing use that sales price. Else, we'd default to the default item sales price. 
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

Title: Batch Reassign Customers - Batch Assigned To Updates
Description:
As employees come and go, I need to make sure my customers are always assigned to an active Sales Rep or Assigned to. 

How can I move accounts from one rep to another?
Solution:
There are multiple flows for this:

1.) A simple export and import could help the customer achieve the desired result.

2.) Alternatively, we could customize this flow:

a.) Loop through all customers with the old 'Assigned To' or 'Sales Rep'
b.) Take note of both the old and new rep
c.) Create an activity to record the account was reassigned
d.) Update the customer

Title: Change Sort Order - Line Order on Transactions
Description:
Ability to change sort order of items on Sales Orders

- He is aware this will be Method:CRM only sort order, and these changes will not reflect the sort order of items in QuickBooks. 

- Sometimes, when creating Sales Orders, a customer requests an item at the end of the order, but the item needs to be ordered/displayed higher up in the items list. 

- Currently, they need to remove all items up to that part, and then add the new item and re-enter the items they removed. 

- They would like to add an item to the middle of a sales order, and have this order reflected internally on the Sales Order screen, print out, as well as in the portal. 

Keywords:
Move Lines
adjust line item
rearrange items
Solution:
This is a pretty common request and quite simple to add.

1. Add MethodLineOrder to Sales Order Lines table
2. Add MethodLineOrder as a column on the New/Edit Sales Order screen
3. Adjust the Sales Order Lines editable grid's sort order to look at MethodLineOrder instead of LineOrder
4. Adjust the Sales Order Line's grid's sort order to look at MethodLineOrder instead of LineOrder
5. On Create Invoice screen, update the Create button's logic to add Invoice Lines following MethodLineOrder instead of LineOrder
6. Update their Sales Order PDF to print the Sales Order Line by MethodLineOrder and not LineOrder
7. If the customer is using Sales Orders in the Customer Portal, make sure to update the grid to sort by MethodLineOrder and not Line Order

The complex part is what the custom may expect when changing the MethodLineOrder. If they are not okay with manually changing every line's MethodLineOrder we then need to ask the following which will balloon the Estimated Time. 

1. When adding a Sales Order Line to an existing Sales Order, do they expect the MethodLineOrder to be added in sequence? Or should they manually enter the MethodLineOrder?
2. What do they expect to happen when deleting a Sales Order Line to an existing Sales Order, then adding a new Sales Order Line?

Title: Invoice Paid Date Field  - Invoice Payment Summary - Invoice Template
Description:
Our customer would like to be able to track the date an invoice was paid on. They use this information for commissions but also internal tracking to understand how much a rep has been paid in a given month or what is currently outstanding. 

Additionally, their customers some times as for a summary of their payments on the invoice when they do partial payments against larger invoices. Ideally it would be nice to show the paid date on the template as well.

Customer requesting to add PAID stamp on invoice print template
Out of Box, Customer is requesting if the invoice print pdf can have PAID stamp similar to what it has on QuickBooks invoices pdf. Currently in Method we have ispaid field with states true for the paid invoices.

Keywords:
Invoice Paid
Add invoice Paid stamp and Balance due on template
Solution:
This is certainly possible.

Using the Recieve Payment applied to transaction table we can update the Invoice:

1.) To show when the invoice was fully paid
2.) We can create a table / relationship from invoice to payments to show how many partials it took to pay the invoice
3.) We can then take this information and display it on the invoice pdf or screens

Title: API Integration - SOS integration
Description: Customer is looking to explore a direct API integration between Method and SOS inventory.
Solution:
Depending on the nature of your needs SOS Inventory Integration & the API Model allows for a number of opportunities to integrate both systems. 

SOS Inventory should have an established and active connection to QuickBooks Online to ensure all three systems are communicating and sharing common fields across the board.

1.) Item Inventory Tracking (by System or by Location)
2.) Sales Order support and fufillment

The above are two common requests we often recieve; however, generally this is implemented in a One-Way Format.

SOS Inventory > Method - On Items & Items by Location
Method to SOS Inventory - On Sales Order Transfer
The opposite could be true; however, with our current experience it's generally better to move from Method to SOS. 

However it depends on the workflow.

Updates after a transaction has been 'Syncd' also creates a number of complex challenges. Customer opting for this integration should expect a considerable amount of time dedicated to development and testing.

One should expect with all integrations, that there will be cases where delays, changes or missing information may occur as a result of workflows or API Changes.

Title: Duplicate Entity - Improve Duplicate Detection - Improve Duplicate Entity Conflict
Description:
We have customers that work together in tandem with QuickBooks, in some cases, they are creating duplicate entities without even knowing. 

For a example, I've created a lead called Method Integration; however, my QuickBooks team has after the fact also created another customer or vendor with the same name Method Integration.

Because the information exists in both QuickBooks and the CRM as different records, there is no easy way to reconcile these duplicates. As a user I don't even know these duplicates exist and are problematic. 

So the CRM user is seeing the Customer Lead, who they can't convert into a customer because of a duplicate conflict. 

And the QuickBooks users is wondering why is there a duplicate. 

This is especially problematic when it comes to customization, and you try and update the 'Customer' table. Because the table will have both entries listed essentially blocking the table from any updates until the duplicate is resolved.

Archived entities further complicate these workflows as well.
Solution: We do have a data clean up catalog available to help resolve this problem. It's about 80-90% effective; however, for archived entities we do need to work with support to rename and bypass the duplicate entity issue.

2.) Work through the process payment action to refund a payment directly from the payment screen. 
3.) On the payment app, have a refund button
4.) Click refund, process refund payment actions, and create a memo to log it.

Title: Invoice Integration with Shopify - Inventory Integration between Shopify and Method
Description:
Customer is currently using Shopify as their primary inventory management tool.

They do not have a full integration with QBooks inventory due to perceived limitations. 

They are wondering what the Zapier or API avenue could look like if they want to connect Shopify and Method directly with a focus on items.

In essence, when a item is added to a Work Order or invoice in Method, can we update Shopify inventory and verse versa.
Solution:
Shopify does not provide any information about items or item levels via zapier.

Therefore this needs to be a direct API integration.
Exploring and implementing any API is generally a large project.

A quick look at the API does look like this is possible:
https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-rest/2023-10/resources/inventorylevel

This wouldn't be a realtime sync but most likely a nightly or daily process.

He will also see this when he logs into his portal which is incorrect. Can you please advise?
Solution:
The customer balance requires an active QuickBooks Connection to be updated at this time:

1.) The customer should check that QuickBooks is connected
2.) The customer should check if the Customer, Invoice, and Payment are not in conflict
3.) Lastly, if everything is working the Customer Balance should update in about 15 seconds.

We certainly can and should improve this experience; however, customization won't be a fool proof solution.

Title: Email & Portal  - Tracking Email (Sent, Opened, Clicked) or Portal Last Accessed Date, Transaction Last Opened, Transaction Last Viewed
Description:
Can the estimate email status be added as a column on the estimate list.
Can the last time a customer accesses the portal be added to the estimate list 

Bo and his team often find estimates landing in Spam folders and this could be a way for them to stay on top of follow-ups and who is engaging with their estimates.

Customer looking to have a feature similar to QuickBooks that allows visibility of when customers view invoices with date and time stamps. They want the date and time visible on Invoice list as well as on Invoice screen.
OB screenshot attached

Keywords:
Estimate Opened
Invoice Opened
Estimate Viewed
Invoice Viewed
Transaction Opened
Solution:
Both as possible.

Portal Tracking is Simple - Create a new field on the Estimate, PortalLastAccessedBy and Date. Everytime the portal screen is loaded update these two fields. 

Email Tracking is Simple as well - Everytime an email is sent. Make sure to assign it a 'BatchID'. eg. TableName+RecordID - Estimate12345

Once a BatchID is set, the following webservice can be called:
https://api.method.me/email/notifications/alocetsystem/stats?

With the parameter batchname

Using the batch name above, you should be able to return the status of the email, Whether it was Sent, Opened, or Clicked.

Screenshots are attached of the webservice configuration.

Title: Gross Profit / Gross Margin - Dynamic on Screen
Description: The customer wants to see the gross margin and profit dynamically on screen as they are creating an estimate or invoice. To make sure they're profitable when creating a transaction.
Solution:
Pretty Simple two new fields can be on the line or transaction basis:

Gross Profit = Amount − Cost
Gross Profit Margin=  ((Amount - Cost) / Amount * 100) 

Pretty easy to calculate these fields are the transaction is created.
​

Title: Duplicate Entity - Improve Duplicate Detection - Improve Duplicate Entity Conflict
Description:
We have customers that work together in tandem with QuickBooks, in some cases, they are creating duplicate entities without even knowing. 

For a example, I've created a lead called Method Integration; however, my QuickBooks team has after the fact also created another customer or vendor with the same name Method Integration.

Because the information exists in both QuickBooks and the CRM as different records, there is no easy way to reconcile these duplicates. As a user I don't even know these duplicates exist and are problematic. 

So the CRM user is seeing the Customer Lead, who they can't convert into a customer because of a duplicate conflict. 

And the QuickBooks users is wondering why is there a duplicate. 

This is especially problematic when it comes to customization, and you try and update the 'Customer' table. Because the table will have both entries listed essentially blocking the table from any updates until the duplicate is resolved.

Archived entities further complicate these workflows as well.
Solution: We do have a data clean up catalog available to help resolve this problem. It's about 80-90% effective; however, for archived entities we do need to work with support to rename and bypass the duplicate entity issue.

Title: API Integration - SOS integration
Description: Customer is looking to explore a direct API integration between Method and SOS inventory.
Solution:
Depending on the nature of your needs SOS Inventory Integration & the API Model allows for a number of opportunities to integrate both systems. 

SOS Inventory should have an established and active connection to QuickBooks Online to ensure all three systems are communicating and sharing common fields across the board.

1.) Item Inventory Tracking (by System or by Location)
2.) Sales Order support and fufillment

The above are two common requests we often recieve; however, generally this is implemented in a One-Way Format.

SOS Inventory > Method - On Items & Items by Location
Method to SOS Inventory - On Sales Order Transfer
The opposite could be true; however, with our current experience it's generally better to move from Method to SOS. 

However it depends on the workflow.

Updates after a transaction has been 'Syncd' also creates a number of complex challenges. Customer opting for this integration should expect a considerable amount of time dedicated to development and testing.

One should expect with all integrations, that there will be cases where delays, changes or missing information may occur as a result of workflows or API Changes.

Title: Whatsup Integration
Description:
On behalf of  Vikram Mehta.

The customer was intrigued by our new SMS function but was wondering if WhatsApp is a customization option for the actual delivery. 

MMS is generally smoother, has read only feedback and provides the customer the option for responses, 2-way communication and reduced cost. 

Currently Whatsup is not available via Zapier. 

Is this possible via API? 

Any Guidance for Whatsup requests in the future?
Solution:
As of my last knowledge sending messages via the WhatsApp Business API is not as straightforward as sending regular messages through the consumer version of WhatsApp. Here are some key points to consider:

WhatsApp Business Account:
To use the WhatsApp Business API, you need to have a WhatsApp Business Account. This is different from a regular WhatsApp account.

WhatsApp Business API Provider:
Businesses can't connect directly to the WhatsApp API. Instead, they need to work with an official WhatsApp Business API provider. These providers are third-party companies authorized by WhatsApp to offer access to the API.

Approval Process:
Before using the WhatsApp Business API, businesses need to go through an approval process with WhatsApp. This involves submitting an application to be reviewed by WhatsApp.

Compliance:
Businesses using the API need to comply with WhatsApp's policies and guidelines. This includes adhering to messaging rules, opt-in requirements, and other policies to prevent spam.

Message Templates:
Messages sent through the WhatsApp Business API often require the use of message templates. These templates need to be pre-approved by WhatsApp and can only be used for specific types of messages, such as transactional or customer service-related communications.

Costs:
Using the WhatsApp Business API is not free, and costs can vary based on the provider, the volume of messages, and other factors.

Technical Integration:
Integrating the WhatsApp Business API into your systems requires technical expertise. You need to set up webhooks, handle message templates, and manage the integration with your business processes.

Security and Data Privacy:
Businesses need to ensure the security and privacy of user data when using the API. Compliance with data protection regulations is crucial.

Use Cases:
WhatsApp Business API is typically designed for medium to large enterprises, and its use cases often involve customer communication, notifications, and support.

Mass Updates / Batch Updates

Title: Progress Invoices or Partial Estimate Invoicing
Description:
Ideally the goal here is to be able to break up an estimate and invoice it as work is completed. 

The customer is open to suggestions on how this can be done for example:

1.) Tracking a % on the item that shows % complete
2.) Invoice line by line.
Solution:
Small to Medium

The workflow to convert an estimate to invoice is straight forward.
The bulk of the work occurs where you're tracking what's been invoices.
How much has been invoiced and how much is remaining.

For example if you only have a single items on an estimate, and that item reads construction. You might want to track the progress as a % and bill for 20% of that line item in 5 increments.

The devil is in the details and how the tracking is completed and reported on.

Title: Add Credit Card Processing Fee to Invoices.
Description:
Currently, the ability to add a convenience fee for paying by credit card is not available in QuickBooks Online. As a workaround, you would need to create a service item for the credit card convenience fee and manually add it as a line item on your customer's invoice to indicate the fee.

Can this be done automatically if a customer choose CC payment on Method Portal?

If this is not possible then can we add something like how we have in our Donor Page App?
Solution:
Yes this can be done in the Customer Portal for when a Customer is paying off an Invoice. 

First we would need a table to hold all the possible Credit Cards that they accept. This table should hold fields like Name, Percentage Amount, Is Active. I would recommend being able to work with this table in either the Invoice Preferences or Payment Preferences.

Next we would need to have this table appear on Guest - View Invoice. We would show a dropdown where they can select which Credit Card they are going to use. On Apply, this will update the Invoice to show a new Invoice Line for the Credit Card charge. The Percentage Amount will work with the Invoice's Subtotal to come up with it's Rate. We would need to add screen logic to Show/Hide these payment options based off if a Credit Card charge has already been applied or if the Balance Remaining = 0.

If you want to apply this to do a Payment for your entire balance or for a bunch of selected Invoices off the Guest - Transaction List screen, this will balloon the time estimate.

Title: Project Management / Project Tracking / Project Tracker App
Description:
Project tracking application creation (functional for desktop and mobile)

Project List screen
i.      Need views to show Project Status (Awaiting Kickoff, In progress, On Hold, Complete and Active (any status other than complete)
ii.      Columns – Project Name, Customer, Type, Status, Start date, End Date, Resource, Hours allocated, Hours remaining, % hours remaining
iii.      Button to add new project

iv.      NOTE: the hours remaining and % hours remaining are calculated –> Hours allocated – Hours used from the Time tracking app

Add/Edit screen
i.      Project name
ii.      Customer drop down
iii.      Area for details
iv.      Type drop down (Audit, Block of Hours, Consulting)
v.      Hours allocated
vi.      Resource Assigned drop down (Employees)
vii.      Start and end dates
 viii.      Project Status

The Project Tracking app and the Time tracking app need to be connected. 
i.      Time tracking app screen

Remove the timer option – we always enter the time manually
Remove the Service Item
Add the Project to tie it to the Project App
Total hours used has to be fed to the project tracking app for the remaining hours calculation  
 

Opportunities customization:
Add a field for Gross Profit and add to reporting (we always look at Revenue and GP)
Customize Opps reports to include Totals for the Month
i.      Total Rev and Total GP
ii.      Total Rev and Total GP by account manager
iii.      Total Rev and Total GP by product
iv.      Total Rev and Total GP by customer
Solution:
In theory, we can likely re-use / re-purpose the opportunity app to do many if not all of these functions. This will also bring down the total cost of the project that is estimated.

However, for the full scale project below is a breakdown. 

Project Tracking App: 
-  This is mostly manual entry (I.e. create a table with the necessary fields, design screens for usability) 
- Estimate: 5-10 hours

Time Tracking: 
- Simply removing things from the screen however TT entires do sync with QB and from memory Service Item was required 
- Add dropdown to TT table and use for time retrieval in Project app 
- Estimate 2-3 hours

Opportunity Reports (assuming this the reports screen and not print reports)
- Would need a loop to iterate through every item and and a separate loop for every account manager and yet another for every customer to update individual revenue and GP numbers, can be timely unless run as an app routine and is only accurate as of the day prior
- Depending on what is used for the profit calculations, time can vary in difficulty. Is this simply Sum Invoices - Sum Bills or do they use other costing metrics (some of which may need manual entry or separate calculations I.e. commissions)
- Estimate 3-5 hours (really depending on how they calculate profit)

Title: Admin Improvements - Ability to reset user password with admin permission - Ability to Change Signin Email
Description:
The customer would  like the ability to reset a users password and send them the reset email if they are the admin.

This was a phone call, they originally asked if they could reset in which I explained even if you were an admin the user would have to reset themselves.  He thought that didn't make sense since he was admin and asked me submit a feature request on his behalf.

Keywords:
Reset Signin Email
Reset Password
Reset Email
Reset Sign in Email
Solution: The answer to this question is not straightforward and depends on the context of the system. However, in general, it is reasonable to give the admin user a way to reset the user’s password, e.g., triggering an email to the user to allow them to log on and enter a new password, or generating a new password for the user and emailing it to the user 123. The admin should not be able to view the user’s current password, as it is supposed to be a secret. It is also important to note that the user should be notified of the password reset and the action should be logged along with the identity of the admin user 1.

Ability to change sort order of items on Sales Orders

- He is aware this will be Method:CRM only sort order, and these changes will not reflect the sort order of items in QuickBooks. 

- Sometimes, when creating Sales Orders, a customer requests an item at the end of the order, but the item needs to be ordered/displayed higher up in the items list. 

- Currently, they need to remove all items up to that part, and then add the new item and re-enter the items they removed. 

- They would like to add an item to the middle of a sales order, and have this order reflected internally on the Sales Order screen, print out, as well as in the portal. 

Keywords:
Move Lines
adjust line item
rearrange items
Solution:
This is a pretty common request and quite simple to add.

1. Add MethodLineOrder to Sales Order Lines table
2. Add MethodLineOrder as a column on the New/Edit Sales Order screen
3. Adjust the Sales Order Lines editable grid's sort order to look at MethodLineOrder instead of LineOrder
4. Adjust the Sales Order Line's grid's sort order to look at MethodLineOrder instead of LineOrder
5. On Create Invoice screen, update the Create button's logic to add Invoice Lines following MethodLineOrder instead of LineOrder
6. Update their Sales Order PDF to print the Sales Order Line by MethodLineOrder and not LineOrder
7. If the customer is using Sales Orders in the Customer Portal, make sure to update the grid to sort by MethodLineOrder and not Line Order

The complex part is what the custom may expect when changing the MethodLineOrder. If they are not okay with manually changing every line's MethodLineOrder we then need to ask the following which will balloon the Estimated Time. 

1. When adding a Sales Order Line to an existing Sales Order, do they expect the MethodLineOrder to be added in sequence? Or should they manually enter the MethodLineOrder?
2. What do they expect to happen when deleting a Sales Order Line to an existing Sales Order, then adding a new Sales Order Line?

Title: API Integration - SOS integration
Description: Customer is looking to explore a direct API integration between Method and SOS inventory.
Solution:
Depending on the nature of your needs SOS Inventory Integration & the API Model allows for a number of opportunities to integrate both systems. 

SOS Inventory should have an established and active connection to QuickBooks Online to ensure all three systems are communicating and sharing common fields across the board.

1.) Item Inventory Tracking (by System or by Location)
2.) Sales Order support and fufillment

The above are two common requests we often recieve; however, generally this is implemented in a One-Way Format.

SOS Inventory > Method - On Items & Items by Location
Method to SOS Inventory - On Sales Order Transfer
The opposite could be true; however, with our current experience it's generally better to move from Method to SOS. 

However it depends on the workflow.

Updates after a transaction has been 'Syncd' also creates a number of complex challenges. Customer opting for this integration should expect a considerable amount of time dedicated to development and testing.

One should expect with all integrations, that there will be cases where delays, changes or missing information may occur as a result of workflows or API Changes.

Title: Simple Price Rules / Price Levels (Method Only)
Description:
Custom Method Only Price Rules

1.) Fixed Percentage (% discount on everything)
2.) Quanity Based Discount (based on volume get 'x' price)
3.) Customer Specific Pricing (based on customer & item combination)

*** Note the customer will input pricing and price levels based on their need.
Solution:
Price Rules are complicated

This estimate assumes:
1.) They are not using Price Levels in QBDT 
2.) They are not using Price Rules in QBO (equivalent to Price Levels in QBDT) - We don't sync with these. 

===================

Price Rules can be very basic (Option 1)
1.) Create a custom table where the rules can be managed
2.) Create a screen where the rules can be edited
3.) Setup the format for the rules -- Fixed Percentage (5%, 10% or 20%)
4.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created.
5.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event. 

===================

Price Rules based on Quantity (Option 2)
1.) Create a sub-table to the item table 
2.) Setup the different levels and pricing for the item (0-5, 6-10, 10-20, 20+) 
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the quatity and apply the correct sales price
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

===================

Price Rules based on Item & Customer (Option 3)
1.) Create a sub-table to the customer table with a link to the item table 
2.) Setup the different items  and pricing for the item for the customer
3.) Every time an estimate, sales order, invoice is created we would need to evaluate the custom table see if the selected customer has specialized pricing use that sales price. Else, we'd default to the default item sales price. 
4.) We would need to incorporate this into the item text change event & quantity changes

Title: Add Credit Card Processing Fee to Invoices.
Description:
Currently, the ability to add a convenience fee for paying by credit card is not available in QuickBooks Online. As a workaround, you would need to create a service item for the credit card convenience fee and manually add it as a line item on your customer's invoice to indicate the fee.

Can this be done automatically if a customer choose CC payment on Method Portal?

If this is not possible then can we add something like how we have in our Donor Page App?
Solution:
Yes this can be done in the Customer Portal for when a Customer is paying off an Invoice. 

First we would need a table to hold all the possible Credit Cards that they accept. This table should hold fields like Name, Percentage Amount, Is Active. I would recommend being able to work with this table in either the Invoice Preferences or Payment Preferences.

Next we would need to have this table appear on Guest - View Invoice. We would show a dropdown where they can select which Credit Card they are going to use. On Apply, this will update the Invoice to show a new Invoice Line for the Credit Card charge. The Percentage Amount will work with the Invoice's Subtotal to come up with it's Rate. We would need to add screen logic to Show/Hide these payment options based off if a Credit Card charge has already been applied or if the Balance Remaining = 0.

If you want to apply this to do a Payment for your entire balance or for a bunch of selected Invoices off the Guest - Transaction List screen, this will balloon the time estimate.

Title: Support Google Analytics Tracking / Capturing of UTM parameters on Web to Lead forms
Description:
Google Analytics Tracking ID

The ability to add a tracking ID to our web to lead forms or any public page for that matter.

UTM Parameters

The types of UTM parameters are synonymous with the five available tags you can use for tracking. 
Below you will find each tag, followed by what they mean and an example:

utm_campaign: This names the specific campaign the link was a part of. For example, you could use opening_day_deals or black_friday_sale. Example: utm_campaign=black_friday_sale

utm_source: Use this to identify where the traffic comes from. Possible sources include search engine, newsletter, or other referrals. Example: utm_source=facebook

utm_medium: This indicates what marketing channel directed a visitor to your website, such as email, social media, PPC ads, guest blog or brand partnerships. Example: utm_medium=social

utm_content: This highlights the call to action that worked. Example: CTAs include buy_now, click_here, learn_more, shop_now, or try_demo.

utm_term: Identifies keywords used in PPC ads, such as “dashboard cam” or “eye drops for allergies,” so you can see what most people are looking for when they find your ads. Links with this tag rarely show up in organic searches. When they do, this may indicate a website indexing problem. Example: utm_term=tennis_shoes or 
utm_term=utm_builder
Solution:
This is an experimental recommendation:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yIWkcMvrpC0

Without some product features this might not work.

Title: Email Transactions - Option to Edit Emails before sending
Description:
Many customers have requested we have the ability to edit the template that will be sent off to the customer from a transaction screen. This will allow them to quickly modify the transaction and personalize it to each customer prior to sending it out. It should also allow them to edit the to/ cc/ bcc fields per transactions. 

Please quote how long it would take to implement:

Hello, recently we got the following feedback from account, cmisales:
'This particular issue has been raised by me since before I signed up for Method. The absolute poor integration of email services within the product. There was a ray of hope/progress on Sept 27 when the email service for invoices allowed cc and bcc fields to be accessed.

My need is very simple.
When I want to email a quote, or invoice, or packing slip from a button on one of these screens, I want to transfer control to the method email app that has

the send-to field populated from the invoice contact,

the subject line populated with some standard text along with the quote/invoice/packing slip number. ie....“CMI  xxxxxx”

and the pdf of the quote/invoice/packing slip attached.
With the email app now in control I can choose to change/add send-to addresses, bcc,cc, subject, body, and attachments.

Why isn’t this a standard feature?'

Basically, they want to be able to edit what is being sent for a transaction, before it is sent. 

this is actually a pretty common request, i've always pointed them to PS. 
Whats requested commonly actually is to change the email address the email is going to

so i proposed this feature: 

When the 'Are you sure?' pop up comes up before you send an email from a transaction, it would be cool if there was a button there to click if you'd like to make a quick edit before sending.
A button that will direct you to the send email screen, where the email that was abut to be sent is autoloaded into the fields. 

From there, you can change the fields and send the email from this screen with the changes
Thanks!!!
Solution:
Zach has graciously created a catalog entry for this request.

This allows you to select contacts to email from transactions
Edit the contacts and add new contacts.
Allows you to edit the body of the email.
Allows you to also attach documents and bcc or cc anyone you need.

Please note, that the billable time to implement this is estimate at 5 hours since there are multiple considerations and tests.
Note from Product:
Phil Cote - Sept 26, 2024
A version of this has been added to the stock flows for sales transactions. We're still in the process of gradually extending the functionality to the rest of the apps' email flows, where applicable. Proposals and Purchase Transactions next on deck.

Title: Project Management / Project Tracking / Project Tracker App
Description:
Project tracking application creation (functional for desktop and mobile)

Project List screen
i.      Need views to show Project Status (Awaiting Kickoff, In progress, On Hold, Complete and Active (any status other than complete)
ii.      Columns – Project Name, Customer, Type, Status, Start date, End Date, Resource, Hours allocated, Hours remaining, % hours remaining
iii.      Button to add new project

iv.      NOTE: the hours remaining and % hours remaining are calculated –> Hours allocated – Hours used from the Time tracking app

Add/Edit screen
i.      Project name
ii.      Customer drop down
iii.      Area for details
iv.      Type drop down (Audit, Block of Hours, Consulting)
v.      Hours allocated
vi.      Resource Assigned drop down (Employees)
vii.      Start and end dates
 viii.      Project Status

The Project Tracking app and the Time tracking app need to be connected. 
i.      Time tracking app screen

Remove the timer option – we always enter the time manually
Remove the Service Item
Add the Project to tie it to the Project App
Total hours used has to be fed to the project tracking app for the remaining hours calculation  
 

Opportunities customization:
Add a field for Gross Profit and add to reporting (we always look at Revenue and GP)
Customize Opps reports to include Totals for the Month
i.      Total Rev and Total GP
ii.      Total Rev and Total GP by account manager
iii.      Total Rev and Total GP by product
iv.      Total Rev and Total GP by customer
Solution:
In theory, we can likely re-use / re-purpose the opportunity app to do many if not all of these functions. This will also bring down the total cost of the project that is estimated.

However, for the full scale project below is a breakdown. 

Project Tracking App: 
-  This is mostly manual entry (I.e. create a table with the necessary fields, design screens for usability) 
- Estimate: 5-10 hours

Time Tracking: 
- Simply removing things from the screen however TT entires do sync with QB and from memory Service Item was required 
- Add dropdown to TT table and use for time retrieval in Project app 
- Estimate 2-3 hours

Opportunity Reports (assuming this the reports screen and not print reports)
- Would need a loop to iterate through every item and and a separate loop for every account manager and yet another for every customer to update individual revenue and GP numbers, can be timely unless run as an app routine and is only accurate as of the day prior
- Depending on what is used for the profit calculations, time can vary in difficulty. Is this simply Sum Invoices - Sum Bills or do they use other costing metrics (some of which may need manual entry or separate calculations I.e. commissions)
- Estimate 3-5 hours (really depending on how they calculate profit)

Title: Packing Slips - The ability to Create a Packing Slip for a Transaction
Description:
The customer is just looking to convert an existing Estimate, Invoice, Sales Order or Work Order into an itemized list without any pricing.

This document would be used to attach with shipping to ensure the list of items is included.


Keywords:
Delivery Notes
Shipping Information
Delivery Info
Solution:
The solution here is quite simple, we would:

1.) Create a new button called Print Packing Slip
2.) Create a preference to keep track of the Packing Slip Template
3.) Create a new PDF template based on the transaction and remove all $ amounts from the pdf.

Title: Outlook Calendar - Outlook Integration - Calendar Sync - Support for Outlook Calendar

Title: Show Customer Balance Update in Realtime - Customer / Invoice Balance Not Updating after Payment
Description: My client's balance on the invoice screen is showing 0.00 (I was the one who took his payment so I know he's all set) but his balance under the Customer Details is showing that he still owes the invoice amount. Solution:
The customer balance requires an active QuickBooks Connection to be updated at this time:

1.) The customer should check that QuickBooks is connected
2.) The customer should check if the Customer, Invoice, and Payment are not in conflict
3.) Lastly, if everything is working the Customer Balance should update in about 15 seconds.

We certainly can and should improve this experience; however, customization won't be a fool proof solution.

Title: Project Management / Project Tracking / Project Tracker App
Description:
Project tracking application creation (functional for desktop and mobile)

Project List screen
i.      Need views to show Project Status (Awaiting Kickoff, In progress, On Hold, Complete and Active (any status other than complete)
ii.      Columns – Project Name, Customer, Type, Status, Start date, End Date, Resource, Hours allocated, Hours remaining, % hours remaining
iii.      Button to add new project

iv.      NOTE: the hours remaining and % hours remaining are calculated –> Hours allocated – Hours used from the Time tracking app

Add/Edit screen
i.      Project name
ii.      Customer drop down
iii.      Area for details
iv.      Type drop down (Audit, Block of Hours, Consulting)
v.      Hours allocated
vi.      Resource Assigned drop down (Employees)
vii.      Start and end dates
 viii.      Project Status

The Project Tracking app and the Time tracking app need to be connected. 
i.      Time tracking app screen

Remove the timer option – we always enter the time manually
Remove the Service Item
Add the Project to tie it to the Project App
Total hours used has to be fed to the project tracking app for the remaining hours calculation  
 

Opportunities customization:
Add a field for Gross Profit and add to reporting (we always look at Revenue and GP)
Customize Opps reports to include Totals for the Month
i.      Total Rev and Total GP
ii.      Total Rev and Total GP by account manager
iii.      Total Rev and Total GP by product
iv.      Total Rev and Total GP by customer
Solution:
In theory, we can likely re-use / re-purpose the opportunity app to do many if not all of these functions. This will also bring down the total cost of the project that is estimated.

However, for the full scale project below is a breakdown. 

Project Tracking App: 
-  This is mostly manual entry (I.e. create a table with the necessary fields, design screens for usability) 
- Estimate: 5-10 hours

Time Tracking: 
- Simply removing things from the screen however TT entires do sync with QB and from memory Service Item was required 
- Add dropdown to TT table and use for time retrieval in Project app 
- Estimate 2-3 hours

Opportunity Reports (assuming this the reports screen and not print reports)
- Would need a loop to iterate through every item and and a separate loop for every account manager and yet another for every customer to update individual revenue and GP numbers, can be timely unless run as an app routine and is only accurate as of the day prior
- Depending on what is used for the profit calculations, time can vary in difficulty. Is this simply Sum Invoices - Sum Bills or do they use other costing metrics (some of which may need manual entry or separate calculations I.e. commissions)
- Estimate 3-5 hours (really depending on how they calculate profit)

Title: Add Credit Card Processing Fee to Invoices.
Description:
Currently, the ability to add a convenience fee for paying by credit card is not available in QuickBooks Online. As a workaround, you would need to create a service item for the credit card convenience fee and manually add it as a line item on your customer's invoice to indicate the fee.

Can this be done automatically if a customer choose CC payment on Method Portal?

If this is not possible then can we add something like how we have in our Donor Page App?
Solution:
Yes this can be done in the Customer Portal for when a Customer is paying off an Invoice. 

First we would need a table to hold all the possible Credit Cards that they accept. This table should hold fields like Name, Percentage Amount, Is Active. I would recommend being able to work with this table in either the Invoice Preferences or Payment Preferences.

Next we would need to have this table appear on Guest - View Invoice. We would show a dropdown where they can select which Credit Card they are going to use. On Apply, this will update the Invoice to show a new Invoice Line for the Credit Card charge. The Percentage Amount will work with the Invoice's Subtotal to come up with it's Rate. We would need to add screen logic to Show/Hide these payment options based off if a Credit Card charge has already been applied or if the Balance Remaining = 0.

If you want to apply this to do a Payment for your entire balance or for a bunch of selected Invoices off the Guest - Transaction List screen, this will balloon the time estimate.

Title: Change Sort Order - Line Order on Transactions
Description:
Ability to change sort order of items on Sales Orders

- He is aware this will be Method:CRM only sort order, and these changes will not reflect the sort order of items in QuickBooks. 

- Sometimes, when creating Sales Orders, a customer requests an item at the end of the order, but the item needs to be ordered/displayed higher up in the items list. 

- Currently, they need to remove all items up to that part, and then add the new item and re-enter the items they removed. 

- They would like to add an item to the middle of a sales order, and have this order reflected internally on the Sales Order screen, print out, as well as in the portal. 

Keywords:
Move Lines
adjust line item
rearrange items
Solution:
This is a pretty common request and quite simple to add.

1. Add MethodLineOrder to Sales Order Lines table
2. Add MethodLineOrder as a column on the New/Edit Sales Order screen
3. Adjust the Sales Order Lines editable grid's sort order to look at MethodLineOrder instead of LineOrder
4. Adjust the Sales Order Line's grid's sort order to look at MethodLineOrder instead of LineOrder
5. On Create Invoice screen, update the Create button's logic to add Invoice Lines following MethodLineOrder instead of LineOrder
6. Update their Sales Order PDF to print the Sales Order Line by MethodLineOrder and not LineOrder
7. If the customer is using Sales Orders in the Customer Portal, make sure to update the grid to sort by MethodLineOrder and not Line Order

The complex part is what the custom may expect when changing the MethodLineOrder. If they are not okay with manually changing every line's MethodLineOrder we then need to ask the following which will balloon the Estimated Time. 

1. When adding a Sales Order Line to an existing Sales Order, do they expect the MethodLineOrder to be added in sequence? Or should they manually enter the MethodLineOrder?
2. What do they expect to happen when deleting a Sales Order Line to an existing Sales Order, then adding a new Sales Order Line?

Title: Method Payments - Instant Signup & Collection
Description:
Summary
Enable businesses to sign up for Method Payments and start collecting payments immediately—without complex setup, third-party integrations, or manual configurations.

Problem Statement
Currently, new customers need to go through multiple steps to enable Method Payments, including external verification, manual configuration, and in some cases, third-party integrations. This process creates friction, delays revenue collection, and can discourage adoption.

Proposed Solution
One-Step Signup & Activation: Allow customers to enable Method Payments directly within Method:CRM with minimal input—e.g., business details, bank info, and tax ID.
Instant Payment Collection: Once enabled, customers can immediately create invoices, accept payments via credit card or ACH, and reconcile transactions within Method:CRM.
No Third-Party Requirements: Eliminate the need for external payment processors or plugins—everything should work natively within Method Payments.
Smart Defaults & Automation: Pre-configured settings for payment terms, auto-reconciliation, and deposit tracking to reduce manual setup.

Compliance & Security Built-In: Ensure compliance with PCI-DSS standards while providing fraud protection and secure payment handling.

User Benefits
✅ Faster Revenue Collection – Businesses can start accepting payments immediately, reducing time-to-cash.
✅ Simplified Onboarding – No complex configurations or integrations needed.
✅ Increased Adoption – Lowering friction will encourage more customers to use Method Payments.
✅ Improved Customer Experience – Reduces frustration by streamlining the payment process.
Solution: Cannot be customized at this time; however, is up for consideration with the product team.

Title: QuickBooks Sync Improvement - Flat Discount Rate on Transactions - Fixed Discount Rate

So something that should be $20.00 off might round down to $19.98 because the % discount field only supports 2 decimal places.

Title: API Integration with UPS  - Estimated Shipping Cost - Package Tracking

Depending on the nature of what needs to be captured and how one should plan for a minimum of 5-10 hours to explore the API and capabilities.

Title: Customer Statements - The ability to create and share a customer statement
Description:
A statement is a summary of your customer's account, listing recent invoices, credit memos, and payments received. A reminder statement can be created and sent to your customers to show how much they owe on each invoice.

Creating a customer statement is a common workflow our business has there may be a lot of payments, invoices, and credits over a short period of time. It enables us to set a start an end date, and show what was the opening balance, what happened on the customer account over a period of time eg. from May 1st to May 31st. Giving the customer a closing balance and the number of invoices that are pending or are coming to their term.
Solution:
A report of this nature is possible to be customized; however, in most cases it's best to recommend the usage of the transaction portal. 

As this is a  live view of their account, including but not limited to showing the current open balance, a summary of all invoices, payments and credit memos and more.

This enables the customer to also review transactions in detail and making payments.

Title: Packing Slips - The ability to Create a Packing Slip for a Transaction
Description:
The customer is just looking to convert an existing Estimate, Invoice, Sales Order or Work Order into an itemized list without any pricing.

This document would be used to attach with shipping to ensure the list of items is included.


Keywords:
Delivery Notes
Shipping Information
Delivery Info
Solution:
The solution here is quite simple, we would:

1.) Create a new button called Print Packing Slip
2.) Create a preference to keep track of the Packing Slip Template
3.) Create a new PDF template based on the transaction and remove all $ amounts from the pdf.

Title: API Integration - SOS integration
Description: Customer is looking to explore a direct API integration between Method and SOS inventory.
Solution:
Depending on the nature of your needs SOS Inventory Integration & the API Model allows for a number of opportunities to integrate both systems. 

SOS Inventory should have an established and active connection to QuickBooks Online to ensure all three systems are communicating and sharing common fields across the board.

1.) Item Inventory Tracking (by System or by Location)
2.) Sales Order support and fufillment

The above are two common requests we often recieve; however, generally this is implemented in a One-Way Format.

SOS Inventory > Method - On Items & Items by Location
Method to SOS Inventory - On Sales Order Transfer
The opposite could be true; however, with our current experience it's generally better to move from Method to SOS. 

However it depends on the workflow.

Updates after a transaction has been 'Syncd' also creates a number of complex challenges. Customer opting for this integration should expect a considerable amount of time dedicated to development and testing.

One should expect with all integrations, that there will be cases where delays, changes or missing information may occur as a result of workflows or API Changes.

Title: API Integration - Woo Commerce - WooCommerce
Description:
Customer is looking to tie his website store which is using WooCommerce together with Method.
Ideally the orders would flow from WooCommerce, into Method, into QuickBooks. 

Having the three systems reporting on the same information would be ideal and big time save.
Solution:
API integrations always take a considerable amount of work and testing.

The scope of an ecommerce integration could be Medium through to Extra Large. 
Woo Commerce does have an open and accessible API which should enable our team to access information like orders and link them to QuickBooks via Method.

That said, there are always compromises to these integrations, depending on the needs of the customer, it's best to review the scope of their needs and determine a plan of action.
